title: Google commits $40 million in AI access to the Genesis Mission
friendlyTitle: Google gives US science labs $40 million of AI access
summary: Google is committing $40 million in AI tokens and cloud credits to US Department of Energy research under the Genesis Mission. Awardees will receive access to tools including AlphaEvolve, AlphaFold 3, AlphaGenome, WeatherNext and AlphaEarth Foundations, while national-laboratory staff will receive Gemini for Government access for one year.
friendlySummary: Researchers will get cloud credits and access to Google tools for biology, weather, Earth mapping, algorithms and everyday laboratory work.
whyItMatters: This puts specialist AI tools directly into scientific and laboratory workflows rather than treating AI as a general chatbot. The value will depend on whether researchers can reproduce gains, manage sensitive data and keep access sustainable after the initial credits expire.
friendlyWhyItMatters: These tools may help scientists test ideas faster, but the results still need careful checking and long-term funding.
